title: Day 13: Mojtaba Khamenei Issues First Statement — Vows Hormuz Stays Closed; Oil Hits $100
detail: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued his purported first statement since succeeding his father (killed Feb 28), calling for unity and declaring the Strait of Hormuz will remain a 'tool of pressure.' He called for all US bases in the region to immediately close or face attack, and warned Iran is studying expansion of the war to additional fronts — citing Hezbollah, Houthis, and Iraqi militias. Khamenei did not appear in the broadcast; a photo was shown on screen while another person read the remarks, raising authenticity questions. Oil prices spiked past $100/bbl overnight in response. The statement contradicted President Pezeshkian's conditional peace terms outlined earlier in the week.

title: IRGC+Hezbollah Joint Attack: 50+ Israeli Targets Across 5-Hour Barrage
detail: The IRGC confirmed a joint operation with Hezbollah striking more than 50 targets across Israel over five hours on March 12. Hezbollah launched its biggest rocket attack against Israel since the war began — heavy volleys toward northern Israel overnight repeatedly driving residents into shelters. Israel responded with airstrikes against Hezbollah launch sites and command infrastructure in Lebanon. Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz issued a direct warning to Lebanon's president: 'If the Lebanese government cannot control the territory and prevent Hezbollah from threatening northern communities and firing at Israel, we will take the territory and do it ourselves.' Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam stated: 'It is a war we did not want. We cannot accept in any way that Lebanon will return to being an open arena for others' wars.'

title: 6 Commercial Vessels Hit in Persian Gulf Over 48 Hours; ~500 Tankers Stranded
detail: Iran struck six commercial vessels in the Persian Gulf over March 11-12, intensifying its maritime blockade. Three more ships were struck overnight March 11-12, including a container ship off the UAE coast and a US-owned tanker near Iraq's southern ports — the first strike in Iraqi waters since the war began. Iran claimed responsibility for striking the US-owned tanker. Separately, emergency crews in Oman battled a fire at fuel storage tanks after a social media video appeared to show an Iranian drone hit. Bahrain also reported Iranian attacks targeting fuel tanks in the country's north. Tracking data showed ~500 oil tankers stranded in the Persian Gulf with only 5 departing the area in 24 hours. IRGC warned: 'Get ready for oil to be $200 a barrel.'

title: IEA Releases Record 400M Barrels; US Contributes 172M From SPR — Oil Still Above $100
detail: The International Energy Agency warned that the Iran conflict is causing the 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.' IEA member states agreed to release 400 million barrels of oil — the largest emergency release in history. The US authorized release of 172 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (about 40% of the total), to be released gradually over approximately four months. IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol said the goal is to keep supply flowing as conflict disrupts shipping and energy infrastructure. Despite the announcement, Brent crude spiked past $100/bbl overnight after Mojtaba Khamenei vowed Hormuz would remain closed. Analysts warned stockpile releases can only partially offset prolonged disruption: the strait normally carries ~20M bbl/day (20% of global consumption).

title: Pentagon Opens Formal Investigation Into Minab Girls' School Strike
detail: The Pentagon formally opened an investigation into the missile strike on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab, Iran, which killed at least 165 civilians — most of them children — after a preliminary internal assessment suggested the United States was at fault. The school was located approximately 200ft from an IRGC Navy base. Neither the US nor Israel had initially accepted responsibility; Israel stated it was 'not aware of any US or Israeli strike on a school.' CNN geolocated footage to the school location. The formal investigation announcement came on March 12, Day 13 of the conflict.

title: Israeli Airstrike in Beirut Kills 4 Iranian Diplomats
detail: An Israeli airstrike in Beirut killed four Iranian diplomats. Iran expressed deep anger and demanded justice at the UN Security Council, calling the act a 'terrorist attack.' The strike further escalated tensions in Lebanon amid Hezbollah's involvement in the broader conflict.

title: Iran's President Pezeshkian Outlines Three Peace Conditions — Diverges from Khamenei
detail: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian publicly outlined three conditions Tehran would require to end the war: recognition of Iran's 'legitimate rights,' payment of reparations, and firm international guarantees against future US-Israeli aggression. His statement came the same day as Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's defiant first address, which offered no ceasefire terms and instead vowed revenge and threatened to expand the war. Middle East analysts noted the contradiction as evidence of a possible internal power struggle between Iran's political and religious-military leadership. Pezeshkian stated: 'That we surrender unconditionally is a dream that they must take with themselves to the grave.'
